title: convert max_web_research_loops to int to make sure comparison works
body:
This PR fixes a bug introduced by my previous PR and the getting the max_web_research_loops from the environment variable. I'm not sure why it is a string value when doing the `if state.research_loop_count <= int(configurable.max_web_research_loops):` compare, I fixed it with just another `int()` around it.

Sorry for that!

step_plan.summary: Ensure `max_web_research_loops` is consistently treated as an integer when sourced from environment/UI configuration, preventing broken numeric comparisons in research-loop routing. The fix normalizes the type in configuration parsing and adds a defensive cast in the routing condition as a backstop.

steps.operation: Normalize `max_web_research_loops` to an `int` at the configuration boundary (and fall back safely on parse errors)
steps.reason: `os.environ.get(...)` and many external config sources yield strings; coercing here guarantees `Configuration.max_web_research_loops` is an `int` everywhere downstream (including LangGraph Studio overrides), preventing type-mismatch bugs during numeric comparisons.
steps.side_effects: If a non-numeric value is provided (e.g., via env/UI), the code must choose a behavior (raise vs. default). Defaulting may hide misconfiguration; raising may break runtime earlier but is clearer. Any callers assuming the raw string will no longer receive it (expected to be correct given the type annotation).

steps.operation: Defensively cast `configurable.max_web_research_loops` to `int` at the comparison site in routing logic
steps.reason: Even with boundary normalization, routing is a critical control point; a local defensive `int(...)` ensures `state.research_loop_count` (int) is always compared to an int, avoiding runtime errors or incorrect branching if configuration typing regresses or bypasses `from_runnable_config`.
steps.side_effects: If `max_web_research_loops` is not parseable, routing may raise at runtime unless handled; consider consistent handling with `from_runnable_config` (e.g., fallback/default) to avoid unexpected crashes. May slightly change behavior by making previously-invalid string values fail fast or be coerced.
